name: litellm-chat-for-vscode
displayName: LiteLLM chat for Visual Studio Code
description: An extension that integrates LiteLLM into Visual Studio Code

chatParticipants.name: litellm
chatParticipants.description: Chat with LiteLLM directly when the VS Code model picker is unavailable.

commands.title: LiteLLM: Toggle Auto-Apply Code Edits (Fallback Chat)
LiteLLM: Approve Pending Tool Call (Fallback Chat)
LiteLLM: Reject Pending Tool Call (Fallback Chat)
LiteLLM: Enable Session Tool Auto-Approve (Fallback Chat)
LiteLLM: Show Tool Call Status (Fallback Chat)

litellm-vscode-chat.defaultMaxOutputToke.description: Default maximum output tokens when not provided by model info

litellm-vscode-chat.defaultContextLength.description: Default context length when not provided by model info

litellm-vscode-chat.defaultMaxInputToken.description: Default maximum input tokens (if null, calculated as contextLength - maxOutputTokens)

litellm-vscode-chat.promptCaching.enable.description: Enable prompt caching for supported models (currently Anthropic Claude). Caches the system prompt to reduce costs and improve response times. Only affects models that advertise prompt caching support.

litellm-vscode-chat.modelParameters.description: Model-specific request parameters to customize API behavior. Supports max_tokens, temperature, top_p, frequency_penalty, presence_penalty, stop, etc. Uses longest prefix matching (e.g., 'gpt-4' matches 'gpt-4-turbo:openai').

litellm-vscode-chat.stopSequences.description: Global fallback stop sequences. Used when a request does not provide modelOptions.stop and modelParameters.stop. Useful for fallback chat (@litellm) where runtime stop options may be unavailable.

litellm-vscode-chat.fallbackModelOptions.description: Fallback-chat-only model options for @litellm. Uses longest prefix matching with fallback model ID normalization (strips :cheapest/:fastest/provider suffixes) and Claude aliases such as claude-code-haiku-4-5, claude-code-sonnet-4-6, claude-code-opus-4-6.

litellm-vscode-chat.fallbackWorkflowStat.description: Enable persistent fallback workflow goal/state injection for @litellm chats. Supports slash commands like /goal, /note, /show-state, and /clear-state.
Maximum number of persistent fallback workflow notes to keep. Older notes are discarded when limit is exceeded.
Maximum number of fallback autonomous-loop checkpoints to retain. Older checkpoints are discarded when limit is exceeded.
Maximum characters stored per checkpoint summary for fallback autonomous loops.
Require explicit /loop-approve between fallback autonomous loop steps.

litellm-vscode-chat.fallbackContextRetri.description: Enable lightweight ranked workspace context retrieval for fallback prompts.
Glob used when searching candidate files for fallback ranked context retrieval.
Maximum files to consider during fallback context retrieval ranking.
Maximum ranked files to include in fallback prompt context.
Maximum snippet lines per ranked file in fallback prompt context.
Maximum total characters for injected ranked workspace context in fallback prompts.
Maximum number of matched symbols to include in fallback symbol-aware context.
Maximum references to include per matched symbol in fallback context.
Include import/export-based file relationship hints in fallback context.

litellm-vscode-chat.fallbackToolCalling..description: Enable tool calling in fallback chat (@litellm participants).
Fallback tool selection mode: auto (optional tools), required (force one tool call), or none (disable tools).
Tool name to force when fallbackToolCalling.mode is required.
Require explicit /tool-approve before executing tool calls from the model.
Maximum number of tool calls to retain in fallback workflow state.
Maximum characters of tool execution output to display in chat.

litellm-vscode-chat.toolLoop.defaultStep.description: Default maximum number of tool steps per tool loop session. Can be overridden per-session with /tool-loop start --steps N.

litellm-vscode-chat.toolLoop.defaultRetr.description: Default maximum consecutive tool failures before the tool loop stops. Can be overridden per-session with /tool-loop start --retries K.

litellm-vscode-chat.toolLoop.defaultChec.description: Default number of steps between tool loop approval checkpoints. Set to 0 to disable checkpoints. Can be overridden per-session with /tool-loop start --checkpoint M.

litellm-vscode-chat.fallbackSafety.maxPr.description: Maximum characters allowed for a packaged fallback prompt after context injection.

litellm-vscode-chat.fallbackSafety.maxHi.description: Maximum number of chat history turns included in fallback request packaging.

litellm-vscode-chat.fallbackSafety.maxRe.description: Maximum buffered response characters kept while parsing streamed fallback output.

litellm-vscode-chat.fallbackSafety.maxSt.description: Maximum characters accepted from a single streamed response chunk before clipping.

litellm-vscode-chat.fallbackSafety.maxLo.description: Hard upper ceiling for fallback tool-loop steps, even when a higher value is requested.

litellm-vscode-chat.autoApplyCodeEdits.description: Automatically apply code edits from fallback chat (@litellm) responses. When enabled, code blocks are applied to detected target files when possible (or to the active file for a first untargeted block), otherwise to a new untitled editor. Use the 'Toggle Auto-Apply Code Edits' command to enable/disable during a session.

litellm-vscode-chat.fallbackEditPolicy.p.description: Require fallback edits to declare a file path before they can be staged or auto-applied. Untargeted code blocks are skipped when enabled.

litellm-vscode-chat.fallbackEditPolicy.s.description: Restrict fallback edits to the current active file only. Structured edits and auto-applied code blocks targeting other files are rejected.

litellm-vscode-chat.fallbackEditPolicy.w.description: Block fallback edits that target files outside the current workspace folder.

litellm-vscode-chat.fallbackEditPolicy.m.description: Maximum number of files a single fallback response may stage or auto-apply. If exceeded, the response's edits are blocked.

scripts.sync:vscode-api: dts dev && dts main && node -e "const fs = require('fs'); if (fs.existsSync('vscode.d.ts')) { fs.renameSync('vscode.d.ts', 'src/vscode.d.ts'); console.log('Moved vscode.d.ts to src/'); }"
